column1: Today's rain is really only the beginning. Lots more is on the way with flooding likely in some spots. A Flood Watch has been issued for part of the region through Monday. Update on the way.

Spring Hill here and we just had the strongest wind gusts come through and rip off my zip ties from my sunshade. It's been up for months and through many of our daily storms. These gusts were different- trust me. If this is any indication of what Deb is bringing with her, these outer bands could get really nasty.
just had the same here in north hudson
Not much going on here in my part of Fort Myers.
I’m here in Gainesville. It looks like we are going to get nailed. We’ve been through many storms and hurricanes. I always hope we don’t lose power. If we don’t I will be a happy camper 🙏🏻❤️
Gainesville here too, and I concur!!

I’m in Port Orange (just south of Daytona) and we have some decent wind but Denis Phillips from all I can see we aren’t even in the cone, so will we get storms or just rain? All my apps are high % for rain.

Hi Jami, 🙋‍♀️, I'm in Port Orange as well. According to Meteorologist Noah Bergren we are only supposed to get 5 inches of rain here in the Daytona Beach vicinity. With that being said do you happen to know when the rain is going to start? Is it supposed to start tomorrow morning, afternoon or evening? I *know* when the storm pulls away and heads up into GA , we are at a HIGH risk for spin up tornadoes. However I think our exact forecast as far as what to expect *may* change before then.
Views from Clearwater Beach around 1pm today
bet the waves are awesome
surprisingly the water was very calm all things considered.
It's pretty cool now. Crazy people still in the water
This was facing NW. We were surrounded by storms but nothing came down for an additional 45 minutes.

look at those peeps staying in the water?!?!
😳🙄
that's crazy I'm 15 minutes from there and we got nothing
Crazy thunderstorms in Spring Hill
It just started raining here in the Shady Hills area about 15 minutes ago, and thankfully we haven't had anything but a light shower in the past 5 days.
Well end of summer pool party has been moved indoors
Finished draining some water out of my pool just as today's deluge started in Odessa 😳

smart
i meant to do that and didn’t yet.

I’m contemplating doing the same. This current deluge of water is getting me near the top again. When it stops I guess I will pull the hose out to drain. Odessa is getting it hard today for the 3rd time.

I left the water level 5" from the top of my coping. I have a deck drain, so it can overflow. If we get as much rain as forecasted, it will overflow and I'll still need to drain it once it stops raining. No worries! Good luck with yours!
Just drained 5” of water
I wish I had automatic drain.
How are you draining water from your pool. This is my first with an inground.

I don't know what your setup is but I just attach a hose to the spigot on top of my pool pump and run the hose toward the back of my yard. Then, with the pump ON, I turn the spigot on. Just don't forget it's on! 😆
That storm came in like a wrecking ball in north Lakeland! We lost power as well, at 1:09 and as I speak, we just got it back at 2:20!
that lightning 🙏
what u didn’t hear was F**K and then me and husband laughing lol didn’t figure they’d let me post that 😂
yes Lakeland got a little scary the wind was crazy
it sure was Squirrely for a bit. We were without power for a little more than hour.
Please advise on what Gainesville should expect and when -- trying to get my Gator motivated to prepare/food shop today for Sunday - Wednesday

😆 Just did an online order with my Knight and set him to Publix to pick it up. Dude. Not your first storm, wouldn't you rather get your groceries now than wade through the parking lot in torrential rain later?
he already has plenty of times!🙄🙄🙄
Thanks for the updates makes things easier to handle
Drained the pool down a little as the last thing I want to do is be out there in the middle of the storm. I learned during Ian that while it may be self regulating it can only keep up with so much
Same here.
if you keep pump on you can’t drain it below skimmer level, right? Which means, for me anyway, going out every few hours (as long as pump is on) and trying to drain it—even during intense rainfall.
You're correct. I dread going out in this storm. TWC for our area says continuous rain until Tuesday evening 😡

I expect to drain our pool continuously until Tuesday evening.
Do you keep your pump on during storm? Mine shuts off at 5 pm so unless I reschedule it I can’t continue to drain it. How do you manage it?
Our pump runs from 7am until 8pm. We might reschedule for 24 hours just to keep up with the draining.

If the water levels become too high, it can burn out the pump motor.
We already drained about 3" today.
Great idea. I’m going to reschedule now too so I can attempt to go out and drain it during night. Not fun. We have a firehouse attached and have to literally be outside to manage it.

We have the same hose and have to stay with it when we drain. This storm is going to produce a lot of rain and wind. I need to get some more batteries for our lanterns since our area easily loses electricity.
I wish you good luck.
I wish the same back to you! Let’s get thru this!!! (And then I’m selling house and never getting a pool again. 😎)
Really isn't a bad idea lol.

I'm going to check my canal and see if they lowered it. I can't get another flood. I had 4 ft. and lost everything. Everything. Car, Furniture. It all went.

its been really raining out here in Carrollwood where I am. On and off downpours then it stops sun comes out then starts all over. I have 2 sisters that live SOG near the Base. Hoping that this storm doesn't cause problems for them. We are high out here but still a bit concerned especially wind.
I grew up in South Tampa off Westshore and Bay Court. Knowing that area there's always flooding there. I bet Bayshore will be under water tomorrow. Noticed you are friends with Susie, she actually lived next door to my parents quite a few years ago. Stay safe!

I grew up down here as well. We were military brats. Lived off of Dale Mabry and Iowa. Now I'm in a condo right at Gandy and Westshore. The flooding isn't as bad now, with all of those apartments down Bridge St., but we still get some. Always an adventure down here. LOL. Yes I know Susie. We used to vacation at the beach the same week every year, and her daughter played softball with my niece back in the day.

that's a coincidence. I also used to work with Susie's sister Becky years back at a medical physician group out here on Carrollwood. My parents are both gone now. Miss them so much. Growing up in South Tampa knew all the areas that flood like Henderson and Dale Mabry and Lois in areas. My Father in law used to live off Westshore and Bay to Bay off Dundee towards the back it always flooded there but never got into his home. He's gone too.
We like Carrollwood been out here since 85. We are off Ehrlich near Veteran's.
I have another sister too she lives out off Valrico off a private road with lots of acreage which is owned by her husband's family. It actually floods out there near their home in one area. Stay safe!
summer downpour. stuck in the car at publix for a pubsub . 5 min and we are good to go
